task_completion.description: Verify that the food.gdt dataset is loaded in the statistical software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gretl main window (or similar software) should be visible, displaying 'food.gdt' as the active dataset and listing 'food_exp' and 'income' in the variable list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'high_income' dummy variable was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The variable 'high_income' should appear in the software's variable list alongside 'food_exp' and 'income'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a two-sample t-test was performed comparing food_exp between the high_income groups.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An output window showing the results of a two-sample t-test (or difference in means) for food_exp grouped by high_income. Look for a t-statistic of approximately -8.887 and 38 degrees of freedom.

task_completion.description: Verify that the t-test results were saved to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, text editor, or save dialog showing 'ttest_results.txt' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that a boxplot comparing food_exp by high_income was generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph window showing a boxplot with two distinct boxes (representing the high_income groups: 0 and 1) for the food_exp variable.

task_completion.description: Verify that the boxplot was saved as an image file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, image viewer, or save dialog showing 'food_boxplot.png' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used statistical software (like Gretl) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Gretl application GUI or console being used, rather than just Linux terminal commands echoing text to create fake output files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just copy-paste the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The t-test results should be formatted in the software's standard output style and contain the exact calculated values (t = -8.887, df = 38), not manually typed into a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the boxplot is a genuine output generated from the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The boxplot must show the specific data distribution (means around 186 and 321) and have the software's native plotting styling (e.g., gnuplot for Gretl), not a generic placeholder image downloaded from the internet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: food.gdt (40 observations). Median income: 13.29. Low-income group (n=20) mean food_exp: $186.44. High-income group (n=20) mean food_exp: $321.76. Two-sample t-test results: t-statistic = -8.887250, p-value < 0.000001, degrees of freedom = 38. The boxplot should display two boxes corresponding to the high_income groups (0 and 1).
